title: 1.1: Registers the fileext keyword with name "fileext" in the sigmatch_table

annotations.description: Verifies that the fileext keyword is registered in Suricata's detection keyword table so that rule writers can continue to use fileext: in their signatures.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch adds sigmatch_table[DETECT_FILEEXT].name = "fileext" and associated registration entries (Setup, flags, alternative) in src/detect-filename.c (diff lines 398-403).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires the keyword to 'continue to function identically from a rule writer's perspective.' [3: Logical Necessity] In Suricata, detection keywords must be registered in the sigmatch_table to be recognized by the rule parser. Without registration, any rule containing fileext: would fail to parse, which is a functional regression. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must register 'fileext' in the sigmatch_table.

title: 1.2: Configures the fileext keyword with both parsing flags (SIGMATCH_QUOTES_OPTIONAL, SIGMATCH_HANDLE_NEGATION)

annotations.description: Verifies the fileext keyword preserves its original parsing behavior where quoted values are optional and negation is handled by the engine.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch sets sigmatch_table[DETECT_FILEEXT].flags = SIGMATCH_QUOTES_OPTIONAL | SIGMATCH_HANDLE_NEGATION (diff line 402).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires the keyword to 'function identically from a rule writer's perspective.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original detect-fileext.c registered with SIGMATCH_QUOTES_OPTIONAL|SIGMATCH_HANDLE_NEGATION (line 129 of the deleted file). SIGMATCH_QUOTES_OPTIONAL allows both fileext:pdf and fileext:"pdf". SIGMATCH_HANDLE_NEGATION allows fileext:!"pdf". Removing either flag would change the keyword's public parsing behavior and break existing rules. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must set both flags on the fileext registration.

title: 1.3: Implements the fileext setup using content matching infrastructure rather than a custom data structure and FileMatch callback

annotations.description: Verifies the refactored fileext keyword delegates to Suricata's generic content matching pipeline instead of maintaining its own DetectFileextData structure, DetectFileextMatch callback, DetectFileextParse parser, and DetectFileextFree cleanup.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch defines a new static DetectFileextSetup in src/detect-filename.c that calls DetectContentSetup and DetectEngineContentModifierBufferSetup (diff lines 412-452). The registration sets only a Setup callback with no FileMatch or Free callbacks.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the keyword's 'internals should be simplified to reuse the existing content matching capabilities in the codebase rather than maintaining a parallel matching engine.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The prompt points to detect-filename.c as the established pattern, which uses DetectContentSetup and the content matching pipeline for the filename keyword. The old detect-fileext.c maintained four custom functions (Match, Parse, Setup, Free) and a custom struct that the prompt calls 'redundant.' [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must use the content matching infrastructure and eliminate the custom matching logic.

title: 1.4: Sets both file inspection flags (FILE_SIG_NEED_FILE, FILE_SIG_NEED_FILENAME) when the fileext keyword is used in a signature

annotations.description: Verifies that signatures using the fileext keyword have the file inspection flags set, which tell the engine to extract filenames from network traffic for matching.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch sets s->file_flags |= (FILE_SIG_NEED_FILE | FILE_SIG_NEED_FILENAME) in DetectFileextSetup (diff line 419).
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original detect-fileext.c set s->file_flags |= (FILE_SIG_NEED_FILE|FILE_SIG_NEED_FILENAME) in DetectFileextSetup (line 269 of the deleted file). FILE_SIG_NEED_FILE tells the engine to trigger file inspection. FILE_SIG_NEED_FILENAME tells the engine to extract the filename from protocol sessions. [3: Logical Necessity] Without these flags, the engine would never inspect files for rules using fileext, causing the keyword to silently match nothing. This would be a critical functional regression. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must set both flags.

title: 1.5: Ensures the fileext keyword performs case-insensitive matching

annotations.description: Verifies that the refactored fileext keyword matches file extensions regardless of letter casing, preserving the original case-insensitive behavior.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch sets cd->flags |= DETECT_CONTENT_NOCASE in DetectFileextSetup (diff line 439).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires 'function identically from a rule writer's perspective.' A rule fileext:"PDF" must match files ending in .pdf, .PDF, or .Pdf. Without case insensitivity, the keyword's matching semantics would change. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original detect-fileext.c explicitly lowercased the extension in DetectFileextParse using u8_tolower (line 209) and used SCMemcmpLowercase for matching (line 170). Case-insensitive matching is the documented behavior of the fileext keyword. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must implement case-insensitive matching.

title: 1.6: Ensures the fileext match is anchored to the end of the filename

annotations.description: Verifies that the refactored fileext keyword matches extensions only at the end of the filename, not at arbitrary positions within the name.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch sets cd->flags |= DETECT_CONTENT_ENDS_WITH in DetectFileextSetup (diff line 440).
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The original detect-fileext.c computed offset = file->name_len - fileext->len and checked only the trailing portion of the filename (lines 166-170). A file extension is by definition a suffix of the filename. [3: Logical Necessity] Without end-anchoring, fileext:"pdf" would incorrectly match a file named 'pdf_report.exe' because 'pdf' appears in the name. This would be a behavioral regression that produces false positive matches. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must anchor the extension match to the end of the filename.

annotations.description: Verifies that the standalone fileext implementation file containing DetectFileextRegister, DetectFileextMatch, DetectFileextParse, DetectFileextSetup, DetectFileextFree, and DetectFileextData is deleted.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch deletes src/detect-fileext.c entirely (diff lines 41-345, deleted file mode 100644, 299 lines removed).
annotations.justification: [4: Quality Necessity] After the fileext logic is reimplemented using content matching infrastructure, the entire detect-fileext.c becomes dead code containing unused functions and data structures. Keeping it creates confusing duplication. The prompt says 'eliminate this redundant standalone implementation,' but this refers to the redundant logic (custom struct, match, parse, free), not necessarily the file itself—an agent can eliminate the redundant implementation by rewriting the file. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, deleting src/detect-fileext.c is the cleanest approach but not strictly required.

annotations.description: Verifies that the fileext header declaring DetectFileextRegister is deleted, since the public registration function no longer exists as a standalone entry point.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch deletes src/detect-fileext.h entirely (diff lines 346-381, deleted file mode 100644, 30 lines removed).
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] detect-fileext.h declares void DetectFileextRegister(void), which is the sole entry point to the standalone implementation. After the fileext keyword is registered within another module and detect-fileext.c is deleted, this header declares a function that no longer exists. Keeping it is dead code. However, this is conditional on 3.1—if detect-fileext.c is rewritten in place rather than deleted, the header is still needed. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, removing src/detect-fileext.h is required if the file is deleted, but not if it is rewritten in place.

title: 3.3: Removes both fileext entries (detect-fileext.c, detect-fileext.h) from src/Makefile.am

annotations.description: Verifies that the build system no longer references the deleted fileext source and header files in the noinst_HEADERS and libsuricata_c_a_SOURCES lists.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes detect-fileext.h from noinst_HEADERS (diff line 9) and detect-fileext.c from libsuricata_c_a_SOURCES (diff line 17) in src/Makefile.am.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After deleting detect-fileext.c and detect-fileext.h, their entries in Makefile.am reference non-existent files. Leaving them would cause build failures during compilation. The prompt says 'clean up any build system references and registrations that become unnecessary,' but these entries only become unnecessary if the files are deleted (3.1 + 3.2). [5: Conclusion] Therefore, removing these Makefile.am entries is required if the files are deleted, but not if the files are rewritten in place.

title: 3.4: Removes both fileext references (#include "detect-fileext.h", DetectFileextRegister() call) from src/detect-engine-register.c

annotations.description: Verifies that the engine registration file no longer includes the deleted header or calls the obsolete standalone registration function.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes #include "detect-fileext.h" (diff line 29) and the DetectFileextRegister() call (diff line 37) from src/detect-engine-register.c.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After detect-fileext.h is deleted, including it causes a compilation error. After the fileext keyword is registered within another module's registration function, calling DetectFileextRegister() would reference an undefined symbol (linker error) or cause double registration. The prompt says 'clean up any build system references and registrations that become unnecessary,' but these references only become unnecessary if detect-fileext.c is deleted and its registration is moved (3.1). [5: Conclusion] Therefore, removing these references is required if the file is deleted, but not if it is rewritten in place.

title: 4.1: Removes any of the 2 types of unrelated entries (keyword registrations, #include directives) from src/detect-engine-register.c

annotations.description: Detects if the refactoring accidentally removes or modifies any keyword registrations, include directives, or function calls in detect-engine-register.c beyond the fileext-specific #include "detect-fileext.h" and DetectFileextRegister() call.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes only two lines from detect-engine-register.c: #include "detect-fileext.h" (diff line 29) and DetectFileextRegister() (diff line 37). All other includes (detect-filename.h, detect-filestore.h, detect-filemagic.h, etc.) and all other registration calls (DetectFilenameRegister, DetectFilestoreRegister, DetectFilemagicRegister, etc.) remain unchanged.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] src/detect-engine-register.c registers hundreds of detection keywords for the Suricata engine. Accidentally removing or modifying any registration call other than DetectFileextRegister would disable that keyword, causing rules using it to fail. Similarly, removing unrelated includes would cause compilation errors. This is a common refactoring mistake when editing a large registration file. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must only remove fileext-specific entries from detect-engine-register.c.
